raw:
{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.7,
    "R1.2": 0.7,
    "R1.3": 1.0,
    "R1.4": 0.7,
    "R1.5": 0.3,
    "R1.6": 0.3,
    "R1.7": 0.7,
    "RX.1": 1.0,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 1.0,
    "RX.5": 1.0
  },
  "active_set": [
    "R1.1",
    "R1.2",
    "R1.3",
    "R1.4",
    "R1.7",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "When you say 'stronger', should I prioritize (A) the core technical argument (claims, evidence, novelty) or (B) the writing and narrative flow (polish, structure)?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The request to make the work 'stronger' implies a need to sharpen the core contribution claim and its framing.",
    "R1.2": "Strengthening the paper requires verifying the logical chain from the problem setup to the final conclusion.",
    "R1.3": "The core strength of the paper depends on whether the reported empirical results robustly support the central claims.",
    "R1.4": "A 'stronger' paper requires a more robust positioning against prior work to clearly establish its unique contribution.",
    "R1.7": "Strengthening the paper's foundation involves ensuring it correctly and comprehensively engages with the relevant scientific literature.",
    "RX.1": "The request to make the work 'stronger' includes ensuring that the limits and uncertainties of the findings are clearly stated.",
    "RX.2": "A 'stronger' paper is one that is more credible, which requires carefully avoiding any claims that extend beyond the evidence.",
    "RX.3": "The task is to strengthen the provided artifact, not to introduce new research directions or unrelated points.",
    "RX.4": "Strengthening the work must not come at the cost of ignoring or downplaying potential negative consequences of the research.",
    "RX.5": "All proposed changes to strengthen the work must be clearly documented to maintain a traceable edit history."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: When you say 'stronger', should I prioritize (A) the core technical argument (claims, evidence, novelty) or (B) the writing and narrative flow (polish, structure)?

rationale.R1.1: The request to make the work 'stronger' implies a need to sharpen the core contribution claim and its framing.
rationale.R1.2: Strengthening the paper requires verifying the logical chain from the problem setup to the final conclusion.
rationale.R1.3: The core strength of the paper depends on whether the reported empirical results robustly support the central claims.
rationale.R1.4: A 'stronger' paper requires a more robust positioning against prior work to clearly establish its unique contribution.
rationale.R1.7: Strengthening the paper's foundation involves ensuring it correctly and comprehensively engages with the relevant scientific literature.
rationale.RX.1: The request to make the work 'stronger' includes ensuring that the limits and uncertainties of the findings are clearly stated.
rationale.RX.2: A 'stronger' paper is one that is more credible, which requires carefully avoiding any claims that extend beyond the evidence.
rationale.RX.3: The task is to strengthen the provided artifact, not to introduce new research directions or unrelated points.
rationale.RX.4: Strengthening the work must not come at the cost of ignoring or downplaying potential negative consequences of the research.
rationale.RX.5: All proposed changes to strengthen the work must be clearly documented to maintain a traceable edit history.
